text.title: design-tokens-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for design-tokens-basics as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using value-based names for semantic tokens: color-blue-500 as a semantic token name.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The name encodes the value. When the brand colour changes to green, all usages of "blue-500" are semantically wrong even if they render correctly. The semantic abstraction breaks.

failure-mode.description: Flat tokens/ directory with all tokens in a single file or per-category files (colors.json, spacing.json) without layer separation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixing primitives and semantics in one file makes it impossible to swap theme files without touching the full token set. Per-category without layer structure breaks the hierarchy and makes dark mode painful.

failure-mode.description: LLMs invent semantic names that don't appear in the project (color.brand.fancy); require agents to read existing semantic tokens first.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without prompt instruction to read existing tokens, agents default to generic names. Generic names scatter across the codebase and make collaboration difficult. Require agents to propose names matching existing conventions.

failure-mode.description: Agents reference primitives by raw hex inside semantic layers ({$value: "#3b82f6"}) instead of the alias ({value: "{color.blue.500}"}).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hardcoding hex defeats the alias abstraction. If the primitive changes, the semantic token stays hardcoded. Require agents to always use the alias syntax.

failure-mode.description: When generating dark-mode tokens, agents flip lightness without checking contrast — pair every output with axe-core or a manual contrast check.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Flipping color lightness (light → dark) does not guarantee WCAG AA contrast for text. Dark mode must be tested with a contrast checker before merging.

failure-mode.description: Style Dictionary transforms are agent-hostile; agents pick transformGroup: 'web' (deprecated) instead of 'css' / 'js'.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Deprecated transform groups may fail silently or produce incorrect output. Require agents to read the current Style Dictionary docs and use recommended transform groups.

failure-mode.description: For Tailwind output, agents emit nested objects but Tailwind v3 expects flat 'color-action-primary' keys at some layers — verify by running tailwindcss build after generation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tailwind's config shape requirements are unintuitive. Always test Tailwind builds after agents generate token files.

failure-mode.description: W3C DTFM $type values are forgotten on numeric tokens (spacing, dimension); some tools error out without them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: W3C Design Tokens Format Module requires $type for W3C-compliant tokens. Tools like Terrazzo and some Style Dictionary configurations enforce this. Require $type on all tokens.
